entries.description: Two Chromium-based extensions impersonating the AITOPIA AI assistant accumulated 900,000 installations and harvested chat histories from ChatGPT and DeepSeek via DOM scraping. Data is Base64-encoded and exfiltrated every 30 minutes to C2 domains including deepaichats.com and chatsaigpt.com. Microsoft Defender telemetry confirmed activity across 20,000+ enterprise tenants.
entries.attribution: Unattributed

entries.description: North Korean state-sponsored APT group observed using LLMs to parse disclosed vulnerabilities including CVE-2022-30190 (MSDT Follina) to map exploitation paths. Masquerades as South Korean government officials to deliver spearphishing payloads. Uses generative AI to accelerate the reconnaissance-to-exploitation cycle and dynamically generate lookalike C2 domains.
entries.attribution: DPRK state-sponsored, attributed by Microsoft Threat Intelligence (confidence: high)

entries.description: North Korean state-sponsored group operating a large-scale fraudulent remote worker scheme deploying thousands of operatives into Western technology companies. Leverages AI to replace images in stolen identity documents, enhance worker photos, utilize voice-changing software for interviews, generate phishing lures, and produce operational code. Has expanded targeting beyond US companies globally.
entries.attribution: DPRK state-sponsored, attributed by Microsoft Threat Intelligence (confidence: high)

ttps: T1195.001 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies
entries.description: First registry-native self-propagating worm operating at scale within npm. Exploits compromised developer accounts to inject malicious code that spreads through package dependency chains. Wave 1 (September 2025) compromised packages including @ctrl/tinycolor (2.2M weekly downloads). Wave 2 (November 2025) compromised 795 packages with 100M+ lifetime downloads, with @asyncapi/specs as patient zero. Version 2.0 added wiper functionality. Steals npm, GitHub, AWS, GCP credentials.
entries.attribution: Unattributed

entries.description: Novel malware technique discovered by ReversingLabs targeting ML models on Hugging Face. Uses corrupted Pickle files to deliver malware that bypasses Picklescan security scanning. Two proof-of-concept malicious models were identified and removed. Demonstrates viable attack path for arbitrary code execution through AI model distribution platforms.
entries.attribution: Unattributed

ttps: T1195.001 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies
T1552.001 - Unsecured Credentials: Credentials in Files
entries.description: Large-scale developer-targeted supply chain campaign compromising 433 components across Open VSX, npm, GitHub, and VSCode marketplace, including 72 malicious extensions with 9M+ installs. Steals npm/GitHub/cloud/crypto wallet credentials, deploys VNC servers and SOCKS proxies, and self-propagates via harvested developer credentials. Uses obfuscated JavaScript with invisible Unicode, Solana blockchain dead drops, and Google Calendar C2 fallback. Russian locale exclusion suggests Russian-speaking operators.
entries.attribution: Suspected Russian-speaking threat actors based on code analysis (confidence: low)

entries.description: First publicly attributed LLMjacking campaign with commercial marketplace monetization. Executed 35,000 attack sessions between December 2025 and January 2026 targeting exposed LLM and MCP endpoints. Operates silver.inc as a commercial marketplace reselling unauthorized access to 30+ LLM providers at 40-60% discounts. Pivoted from compute theft to MCP reconnaissance for lateral movement into internal systems.
entries.attribution: Attributed to aliases Hecker, Sakuya, and LiveGamer101 by Pillar Security and BleepingComputer (confidence: medium)

ttps: T1027.013 - Obfuscated Files or Information: Unicode Encoding
entries.description: Supply chain attack vector targeting AI coding assistants. Attackers inject hidden malicious instructions into Cursor and GitHub Copilot configuration files using invisible Unicode Tag Characters (U+E0001 to U+E007F). Poisoned rules cause AI to generate code containing vulnerabilities, backdoors, or data exfiltration logic. AgentSeal found 12% of scanned public rules files contained hidden instructions.

ttps: T1213.003 - Data from Information Repositories: Code Repositories
entries.description: Passive prompt injection vulnerability in GitHub Copilot within Codespaces discovered by Orca Security. Attackers embed hidden instructions in GitHub Issues using HTML comment tags. The attack chain exfiltrates GITHUB_TOKEN through symbolic links and schema-based exfiltration, enabling full repository takeover without social engineering. Patched by GitHub following coordinated disclosure.
Jailbroken LLM service marketed for BEC email generation, phishing lure creation, and malware code writing. Built on GPT-J 6B with removed safety guardrails.
Subscription-based malicious AI chatbot for generating phishing pages, writing undetectable malware, and crafting social engineering content.
Telegram-based wrapper around jailbroken LLM providing uncensored AI for malware coding, exploit development, and phishing content generation.
Malware family that leverages LLM prompt injection techniques for dynamic payload generation and C2 command obfuscation.
Spyware targeting AI platform users to exfiltrate prompts, API keys, and conversation histories from ChatGPT, Claude, and other LLM interfaces.

ttps: T1195.001 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies
entries.description: Supply chain malware distributed via trojanized AI/ML Python packages targeting data scientists and ML engineers.
Infostealer and cryptominer distributed through fake AI-powered cryptocurrency trading platforms and AI investment bot schemes.

entries.attribution: APT28 / UAC-0001
entries.description: First publicly documented malware to integrate a live LLM API for dynamic C2 command generation and response parsing. Attributed to APT28, it uses OpenAI-compatible API calls to generate contextually appropriate commands based on host reconnaissance.

ttps: T1195.001 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies
T1102 - C2 via Web Service (ICP blockchain)
T1078 - Valid Accounts (PyPI maintainer credential abuse)
entries.description: TeamPCP is a supply chain-focused threat actor demonstrating sophisticated attack chain design: first compromising Trivy (security scanner) to extract CI/CD credentials, then using those credentials to obtain PyPI maintainer access. The campaign targets AI development ecosystems by poisoning packages at the orchestration layer (LiteLLM bridges 100+ LLM providers). Attack chain includes multi-stage persistence, credential encryption, and beacon-based follow-on payload delivery.
entries.attribution: Attributed by Wiz, Datadog Security Labs, SANS, and affected maintainers (confidence: high)

entries.description: Financially motivated threat group specializing in LLMjacking operations. Steals and resells cloud AI API keys, operates proxy services for unauthorized access to commercial LLM APIs including Azure OpenAI and AWS Bedrock.

distribution: mass exploitation of FortiGate VPN appliances
entries.attribution: Russian-linked actors
entries.description: Mass exploitation campaign targeting FortiGate VPN appliances using GenAI-assisted vulnerability discovery and exploit generation. Demonstrates nation-state adoption of LLMs to accelerate exploitation workflows.

entries.attribution: DPRK / Lazarus Group affiliates
entries.description: North Korean state-sponsored use of LLMs across multiple APT groups including Emerald Sleet, Jasper Sleet, and Coral Sleet. Operations span IT worker fraud schemes with AI-enhanced identity documents, LLM-assisted vulnerability research and exploitation path mapping, jailbroken model use for malware development, and AI-generated phishing lures and operational code. Microsoft 2026 Red Report documents early experimentation with agentic AI for iterative decision-making.

distribution: global distribution via npm package manager
ttps: T1547.015 - Scheduled Task/Job via postinstall Hook
entries.description: UNC1069 is a financially motivated North Korea-nexus threat actor with activity dating to at least 2018, historically using WAVESHAPER backdoor. This group demonstrates sophisticated supply chain attack capabilities, pre-staging malicious dependencies 18 hours in advance, bypassing GitHub Actions OIDC via stolen long-lived npm tokens, and deploying obfuscated cross-platform RATs targeting macOS, Windows, and Linux simultaneously.
entries.attribution: Attributed to UNC1069 by Google Threat Intelligence Group (confidence: high)

entries.description: Cross-platform backdoor with variants for Windows, macOS, and Linux, featuring base64-encoded JSON C2 communications, PE injection capabilities, and script execution functionality
entries.attribution: Linked to UNC1069 operations (confidence: high)

entries.description: Hive0163 is a financially motivated ransomware group tracked by IBM X-Force, associated with Interlock ransomware, NodeSnake, InterlockRAT, and JunkFiction loader. The group employs ClickFix social engineering for initial access and works with initial access brokers. In early 2026, Hive0163 became the first financially motivated group confirmed by a major threat intelligence vendor to deploy likely AI-generated malware (Slopoly) in a live intrusion, indicating the group is actively experimenting with LLM-assisted tooling to accelerate development and generate disposable, hard-to-attribute C2 frameworks.
entries.attribution: Financially motivated cybercrime cluster (confidence: high per IBM X-Force); no nation-state attribution

entries.description: Slopoly is a likely LLM-generated PowerShell backdoor discovered by IBM X-Force in early 2026 during a Hive0163 ransomware engagement. It functions as a basic C2 client, sending JSON heartbeats to a remote server and executing received commands via cmd.exe. Its LLM origin is evidenced by extensive inline comments, clean error handling, and a self-description as a 'Polymorphic C2 Persistence Client' despite lacking polymorphic capabilities. It is dropped to C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows\Runtime\ and persists via a scheduled task named 'Runtime Broker'.
entries.attribution: Attributed to Hive0163 (confidence: high)

entries.description: APT36 (Transparent Tribe) is a Pakistan-linked state-sponsored threat group that has historically targeted Indian government and defence entities using conventional malware tooling. Beginning in late 2025 and accelerating into early 2026, the group pivoted to an AI-assisted 'vibeware' development model, using LLMs to generate high volumes of disposable, polyglot implants in niche languages (Nim, Zig, Crystal, Rust, Go). The strategy — termed 'Distributed Denial of Detection' by Bitdefender — floods targets with simultaneous multi-language implants to overwhelm security engines and ensure persistence even if individual channels are neutralized. C2 is conducted exclusively via legitimate cloud services (Slack, Discord, Supabase, Google Sheets) to evade network controls.
entries.attribution: Pakistan-linked state-sponsored group (confidence: medium per Bitdefender); historically targets Indian government, aerospace, and defence sectors

distribution: Network-based attack via vLLM API endpoint
entries.description: An unknown threat actor exploited a design flaw in nanobot's email processing to execute arbitrary commands via prompt injection, demonstrating that personal AI assistants with email integration create an unauthenticated attack surface.
entries.attribution: Unattributed; exploitation appears widespread but no specific actor attribution available

names: Sigrid Jin and Yeachan Heo (source reconstruction)
entries.description: Unknown actors are likely weaponizing the exposed Claude Code source and the Adversa AI-disclosed permission bypass to craft malicious repository configurations that trick Claude Code users into executing arbitrary commands via prompt injection embedded in CLAUDE.md.
entries.attribution: Unattributed (active exploitation expected post-leak)

entries.description: Anthropic's mistaken inclusion of a sourcemap in the Claude Code npm package led to full source code exposure. The root cause was an unintended debugging artifact in the v2.1.88 release, which was rapidly weaponized by security researchers and attackers to discover and exploit post-leak vulnerabilities.
entries.attribution: Anthropic Internal Process Error

entries.description: A coordinated campaign targeting the nascent MCP ecosystem has embedded malicious instructions directly into tool metadata, impersonated legitimate MCP packages, and exploited proxy vulnerabilities affecting hundreds of thousands of development environments. The attack pattern indicates sophisticated understanding of AI agent architecture and the implicit trust model underlying MCP tool registration.
entries.attribution: Unattributed (likely financially motivated, possibly nation-state experimentation)

entries.description: A breach of Mercor, a leading AI training data vendor, has exposed sensitive information about how major AI labs (Meta, OpenAI) develop and train their models. The incident is under investigation by the affected companies and represents a critical supply-chain vulnerability in the AI development ecosystem.
entries.attribution: Unattributed (motivations unclear: financial, espionage, or insider threat)

entries.description: Threat actors actively exploiting CVE-2025-59528 in Flowise deployments as of April 7, 2026. Attribution remains unclear; exploitation appears opportunistic and widespread rather than targeted campaign-based.
entries.attribution: Unattributed

distribution: Gated access to select defense organizations
AWS, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorgan Chase, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Palo Alto Networks, and 40+ additional organizations
ttps: T1566 - Phishing for Information (potential misuse vector)
entries.description: Claude Mythos Preview represents a watershed moment in AI-assisted cybersecurity: an AI model capable of autonomously identifying exploitable zero-day vulnerabilities at scale and generating working exploits. While Anthropic positioned this as a defensive tool, the technology demonstrates that frontier AI models have reached capability levels that blur the distinction between security tool and offensive weapon. Threat implications: adversarial nations or threat actors with access to comparable models could identify vulnerabilities at speeds that vastly outpace human patching cycles, potentially enabling sustained zero-day campaigns.
entries.attribution: Anthropic (defensive initiative); potential for adversarial misuse by nation-states or APT groups if access compromised
